stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains like transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance to the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of text generation using transformer models.
The model is associated with Meta (implied by 'meta-llama') and is available within the Hugging Face ecosystem.

stories.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: The article covers the high-profile ousting of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.
The reason cited for Altman's removal was a lack of consistent candor in his communications with the board.
Mira Murati's deposition and trial exhibits in Musk v. Altman are providing new insights into the events.

stories.title: SpaceX has a $55 billion plan to build AI chips in Texas
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: SpaceX is planning a massive $55 billion investment into a new AI chip manufacturing facility, 'Terafab', located in Austin, Texas.
This initiative signals Elon Musk's significant push into the highly competitive AI hardware and semiconductor industry.
The plan was detailed in a public hearing notice filed in Grimes, according to reporting from The New York Times and CNBC.

stories.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla
stories.author: George Hammond, Financial Times
bullet_points: Elon Musk attempted to recruit OpenAI founders to establish an internal AI unit within Tesla.
Musk's proposal was conditional, requiring him to gain control over the new for-profit entity.
The story highlights the intense competition and strategic maneuvering within the high-stakes AI development sector.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Existing methods either rely on potentially biased self-citation or use RAG without validating source accessibility or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework utilizing a reproducible AST parser to address this critical verification gap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
This emphasizes a trend toward private, on-device AI execution rather than cloud-based solutions.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple's rumored AirPods featuring cameras for AI applications are reportedly close to early mass production testing.
Prototypes are currently in the design validation test stage, preceding the production validation test stage.
The integrated cameras are not intended for traditional photography but are tied to AI functionality.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method for video generation that allows for fine-grained control over both character motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while enabling per-frame control of camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is designed to build upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician' workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system provides holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is built upon existing image-to-video diffusion models, conditioning them using scene depth and character information.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper challenges the validity of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the best-fit Bradley-Terry ranking is misleading.
The top 50 models ranked by the global metric are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely above 0.53.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Retrieval-Augmented Agents (RAGs), where retrieval is treated as a 'black box'.
Current RAG methods mimic novice searching (iterative query reformulation) rather than expert navigation.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Canvas is down as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
stories.author: stefanpie
bullet_points: Canvas, a major educational technology platform, is reported to be experiencing downtime.
The outage is linked to threats from the hacking group ShinyHunters, who are threatening to leak sensitive school data.
The incident suggests a significant security vulnerability affecting educational institutions relying on the platform.

stories.title: Cloudflare to cut about 20% workforce
stories.author: PriorityLeft
bullet_points: Cloudflare is implementing significant workforce reductions, cutting approximately 20% of its staff.
The announcement is framed within a blog post titled 'Building for the Future,' suggesting strategic restructuring.
This signals a major operational shift within a leading infrastructure/cloud services provider.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function.
Associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its deep integration within the modern ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary AI tools like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Sony’s PS5 sales plummet amid price rises and a memory crisis
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Sony's PS5 sales dropped significantly by 46% year-over-year in the latest fiscal quarter.
The sales decline is attributed to Sony raising the PS5 console price twice in the last year.
The regular PS5 price increased from $499.99 to $649.99.

stories.title: Nintendo is raising Switch 2 prices
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Nintendo is increasing the global price of the Switch 2 console.
The US price will rise from $449.99 to $499.99 starting September 1st.
Nintendo is forecasting a decline in Switch 2 sales over the coming year due to market conditions.

stories.title: Canvas is online again after ShinyHunters threaten to leak schools’ data
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Canvas, the Instructure-owned LMS, experienced a major outage due to a data breach.
The breach exposed sensitive student data, including names, emails, ID numbers, and messages.
The hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for the attack.

stories.title: DHS can’t create vast DNA database to track ICE critics, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the Department of Homeland Security's (DHS) ability to create a massive DNA database.
The legal action specifically alleges that the DHS is integrating a DNA database with ICE surveillance systems.
The story touches on significant privacy and surveillance technology concerns within government agencies.

stories.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Mozilla is adopting AI-assisted bug discovery tools, specifically from Mythos.
The vulnerability scanning tool reported 271 vulnerabilities with a high degree of accuracy ('almost no false positives').
This signals a growing industry trend toward integrating AI into software security and development lifecycles.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly shifting focus or increasing operations at Vandenberg Space Force Base.
Vandenberg is anticipated to become SpaceX's most active launch site in the near term.
The article suggests a potential strategic evolution or change in SpaceX's launch site utilization.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a new concept for a globally shared expert pool in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that the rigid per-layer expert allocation rule might be suboptimal, as random routing shows minimal accuracy degradation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs struggle to generate valid, challenging, and novel mathematical problems needed for advanced training.
The proposed method, VHG, addresses the limitations of existing approaches (human dependency or naive self-play).
VHG utilizes a verifier mechanism to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound, mitigating reward hacking.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in the pretraining and finetuning of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study observes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining improves the learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment auditing.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search pattern.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond black-box retrieval by incorporating expert-like priors to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLMs for long-horizon decision-making in agentic RL.
Introduces StraTA, a framework that integrates explicit trajectory-level strategies into RL agents.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has isolated expert capacity.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy, implying less rigid expert allocation is possible.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate valid, challenging, and novel problems for advanced training.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure generated mathematical problems are sound, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
Relevant to the future of autonomous AI research and the need for high-quality, synthetic data generation in scientific domains.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research proposes that using the same optimizer for full finetuning as was used during pretraining for LLMs leads to better learning-forgetting tradeoffs.
This approach allows models to retain more knowledge from pretraining while achieving comparable or superior performance on new tasks during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).
The findings suggest this method outperforms other optimizers and, surprisingly, LoRA during the SFT stage.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmarks or ground-truth labels exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being overly focused on action recognition, neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) by proposing a novel Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) pretraining method called EMO.
A key challenge in current MoE implementations is that restricting inference to a subset of experts for specific domains causes significant performance degradation.
EMO aims to improve the practical utility of MoEs, allowing for specialized, efficient deployment of LLMs without sacrificing performance.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure generated problems are mathematically sound, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
The research aims to advance LLM training and enable more autonomous scientific research by improving problem generation.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to integrate explicit trajectory-level strategies into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO is designed to train recursive agents capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current LLM reasoning research: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying training scaling.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two crucial axes of difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to systematically improve long-horizon reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the non-trivial problem of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Dirtyfrag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: flipped
bullet_points: The article concerns 'Dirtyfrag,' a vulnerability related to Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE).
LPE vulnerabilities are significant security flaws that allow unauthorized access or elevated permissions on a system.
The source (Hacker News) suggests this is a technical, security-focused topic relevant to the Linux community.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The article discusses a 'Dirty Frag' vulnerability.
This vulnerability is identified as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE).
LPE vulnerabilities are critical security flaws affecting Linux systems.

stories.title: Deterministic Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
stories.author: anna_danilec
bullet_points: LLMs are inherently non-deterministic, meaning identical prompts can yield different outputs.
The article discusses the need for 'deterministic guardrails' to manage this inherent randomness.
This topic is relevant to improving reliability and predictability in AI agent deployment.
